B7 and B8 use variable 'şeker' (sugar) instead of 'enerji' (energy) as required by the worksheet

item_schema.WS3_B1: Part 1: Patlamış Mısır (Popcorn) classification using Leo's fat rule (fat ≤ 8.0g = recommended)
item_schema.WS3_B2: Part 1: Patlamış Mısır reason (fat value vs threshold)
item_schema.WS3_B7: Part 2: Student's own Energy threshold — left branch (≤)
item_schema.WS3_B8: Part 2: Student's own Energy threshold — right branch (>)

raw_ocr.ws_snapshot: Sample_Student eşik uygulamasını doğru yapmış; tüm sınıflandırma sonuçlarını eksiksiz doldurmuş. B7 ve B8'deki eşik ifadelerinde ≤ ve > operatörlerini doğru kullanmış.

warnings: WS3_B2, WS3_B4, WS3_B6: Reason blanks not captured in extraction — these exist on the worksheet but were not separately extracted
WS3_B7: Uses variable 'şeker' (sugar) instead of the required 'enerji' (energy) — variable_mismatch flag

WS3_B1: Raw OCR shows 'Tavsiye edilir' for Patlamış Mısır — answer key expects 'tavsiye edilemez' (popcorn has 23g fat > 8g). This may indicate either (a) student applied sugar rule not fat rule, or (b) extraction captured the wrong blank

student_snapshot.llm_observations: Extraction shows classification labels only. Reason blanks (B2/B4/B6) were not captured. B7/B8 use sugar (şeker) instead of required energy (enerji). B1 classification for popcorn ('tavsiye edilir') contradicts Leo's fat rule — possible that student used their own threshold throughout.

WS3_B1.rationale: Answer key expects 'tavsiye edilemez' for Patlamış Mısır (23g fat > 8.0g = not recommended). Student wrote 'Tavsiye edilir'. This is incorrect for Leo's fat rule. Note: if student applied sugar rule (şeker ≤ 10), classification might differ depending on popcorn's sugar content in the dataset.
WS3_B1.flag: possible_variable_mismatch — student may have applied sugar threshold throughout Part 1, not Leo's fat rule

WS3_B2.rationale: Reason blank not captured in extraction. Cannot score.

WS3_B3.rationale: Apple has 0.2g fat ≤ 8.0g → tavsiye edilebilir. Student wrote 'Tavsiye edilir'. Correct.

WS3_B5.rationale: French fries have 14g fat > 8.0g → tavsiye edilemez. Student wrote 'Tavsiye edilmez'. Correct.

WS3_B7.rationale: Worksheet explicitly instructs: 'Enerji değişkeni için kartların dağılımına uyan bir eşik değeri seçin'. Student used 'şeker' (sugar) not 'enerji' (energy). This is a variable_mismatch error. Operator form (≤) is correct but variable is wrong.

WS3_B8.rationale: Same variable_mismatch as B7. Right branch uses 'şeker' not 'enerji'.

summary.scoring_note: B3 (apple) and B5 (french fries) correct. B1 (popcorn) wrong. All reason blanks (B2/B4/B6) missing from extraction — re-extraction needed. B7/B8 use wrong variable. Caution: B1 error may be a symptom of applying sugar threshold throughout Part 1, not of misunderstanding Leo's rule — this needs clarification.

Acquire_confirmed: WS3_B3: Apple correctly classified — LO3.1.1 (applying threshold rule)
WS3_B5: French fries correctly classified — LO3.1.1
Acquire_missing: WS3_B1: Popcorn classification wrong — application of Leo's fat rule failed
WS3_B7/B8: Own threshold uses wrong variable — cannot confirm LO3.1.3 (operating validated AI tool with correct parameters)

gate_4_aicft.ws_contribution_to_portfolio: WS3 shows partial Acquire — 2 of 3 classifications correct, but popcorn wrong and own threshold uses wrong variable. Re-extraction needed for full assessment. Variable mismatch (sugar vs energy) suggests possible confusion between Part 1 (Leo's fat rule) and Part 2 (student's own energy rule).
